title: campanula rotundifolia subsp. sudetica
intro: Campanula rotundifolia subsp. sudetica is a subspecies of flowering plant in the bellflower family, native to Central Europe, particularly in the Sudeten Mountains of the Czech Republic and Poland. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory problems. It can be prepared as an herbal tea or infused oil, and in some traditional practices, it is also used as a tincture or dried herb for its medicinal properties.
